prompt: Write six sentences about history. Each sentence must contain exactly one number (a digit or written-out number). Not zero numbers, not two, exactly one per sentence.
response:
Here are six sentences about history, each containing exactly one number:

The Great Wall of China was built over many centuries and stretches for thousands of miles across 2 continents' worth of terrain.

Ancient Rome was famously built on seven hills along the banks of the Tiber River.

The French Revolution began in 1789 and dramatically transformed European politics forever.

Queen Victoria ruled the British Empire for sixty-three years during an era of remarkable expansion.

The ancient Library of Alexandria was considered 1 of the greatest centers of learning in the classical world.

World War Two reshaped the entire global order and led to the creation of the United Nations.
